title: De Novo Classification Request for ECG App DEN180044
claim_supported: A representative regulated consumer single-lead ECG app document describes rhythm-screening centered on sinus rhythm and atrial fibrillation; this is a product-specific example rather than a claim about all wearables
notes: Report sections 1.1 and 8.7 use this only for functional-scope background; no sensitivity/specificity or commercial comparison is imported

title: 2018 ACC/AHA/HRS Guideline on Bradycardia and Cardiac Conduction Delay
claim_supported: Ambulatory ECG monitoring duration is selected according to symptom frequency; 24/48-hour Holter and longer modalities are established contexts
notes: Report sections 1.1 2.2 and 8.2 use this only for conservative long-duration motivation; not a performance comparator

title: MIT-BIH Normal Sinus Rhythm Database v1.0.0
claim_supported: NSR-class records originate from fixed version nsrdb 1.0.0; DOI 10.13026/C2NK5R; file license ODC-By 1.0
notes: Raw files are fetched from PhysioNet and not bundled; standard PhysioNet citation required

title: BIDMC Congestive Heart Failure Database v1.0.0
claim_supported: CHF-labelled records originate from fixed version chfdb 1.0.0; DOI 10.13026/C29G60; file license ODC-By 1.0
notes: CHF is a source-dataset label; cite Baim et al. and standard PhysioNet citation

title: MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database v1.0.0
organization_or_authors: George Moody and Roger Mark / PhysioNet
claim_supported: ARR-labelled records originate from fixed version mitdb 1.0.0; the database contains 48 half-hour excerpts selected from a set of 24-hour ambulatory ECG recordings; DOI 10.13026/C2F305; file license ODC-By 1.0
notes: ARR is a broad source-dataset category; the half-hour duration constrains the common four-class evaluation window; cite Moody and Mark 2001 and standard PhysioNet citation

title: MIT-BIH Atrial Fibrillation Database v1.0.0
organization_or_authors: George Moody and Roger Mark / PhysioNet
claim_supported: AF-labelled records originate from fixed version afdb 1.0.0; DOI 10.13026/C2MW2D; file license ODC-By 1.0
notes: Raw files are not bundled; cite Moody and Mark 1983 and standard PhysioNet citation

title: Open Data Commons Attribution License v1.0
claim_supported: Attribution license referenced by each of the four fixed-version PhysioNet dataset pages
notes: Repository does not relicense third-party data and deliberately omits raw waveform files

title: PhysioBank PhysioToolkit and PhysioNet: Components of a New Research Resource for Complex Physiologic Signals
claim_supported: Standard PhysioNet citation requested by the fixed-version dataset pages; RRID:SCR_007345
notes: Use together with dataset-specific original publication where requested

title: ECG Classification Algorithm Based on STDP and R-STDP Neural Networks for Real-Time Monitoring on Ultra Low-Power Personal Wearable Devices
claim_supported: Individual R-peak-centered heartbeat segments are amplitude-to-spike encoded; STDP learns timing features and reward/punishment R-STDP assigns heartbeat classes
notes: Not a long-record or Snapshot-to-record classifier; method cross-checked against author manuscript arXiv:1905.02954

title: Real-Time Ultra-Low Power ECG Anomaly Detection Using an Event-Driven Neuromorphic Processor
organization_or_authors: Felix Christian Bauer / Dylan Richard Muir / Giacomo Indiveri
claim_supported: Asynchronous binary ECG events feed a recurrent SNN reservoir and low-pass readout whose threshold produces a pathological-pattern trigger on DYNAP hardware
notes: Continuous event-driven detection; no multi-disease Snapshot evidence accumulation to record-level class

title: An Event-Driven Compressive Neuromorphic System for Cardiac Arrhythmia Detection
organization_or_authors: Jinbo Chen / Fengshi Tian / Jie Yang / Mohamad Sawan
claim_supported: Level-crossing ADC event output directly feeds an SCNN evaluated for selected MIT-BIH heartbeat classes
notes: Conference publication verified; simulation-based input compression and beat classification rather than long-record aggregation

title: Multiple Instance Learning for ECG Risk Stratification
organization_or_authors: Divya Shanmugam / Davis Blalock / John Guttag
claim_supported: Consecutive-beat instances from approximately 48-hour ECG are scored and the highest-risk 20 percent are averaged into patient-level cardiovascular-death risk
notes: Closest conceptual precedent for sparse pathological instances; binary prognosis software not RTL four-class classification

title: Convolutional Recurrent Neural Networks for Electrocardiogram Classification
organization_or_authors: Martin Zihlmann / Dmytro Perekrestenko / Michael Tschannen
claim_supported: CNN local features from 9-to-61-second ECG are aggregated by temporal averaging or bidirectional LSTM into normal AF other or noisy record class
notes: Shows record-level four-class classification already exists; different classes length software model and implementation

title: Modeling day-long ECG signals to predict heart failure risk with explainable AI
claim_supported: A 30-second window encoder and Transformer sequential head integrate 24-hour Holter features into a five-year HF risk score
notes: DeepHHF publication status re-verified as journal article; binary prognosis software not four-class RTL
